title: taraxacum saevum
intro: Taraxacum saevum, also known as the bitter dandelion or wild dandelion, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its bitter compounds and inulin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Taraxacum saevum include its ability to support liver and digestive health, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve immune function. Taraxacum saevum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote detoxification, reduce inflammation, and improve overall well-being.
